claims.claim: Kuramoto synchronization model exhibits a continuous phase transition from disordered (R~0) to synchronized (R~0.97) states as coupling K increases from 0 to 4.
limitations: K range limited to 0-4; transition may extend beyond observed range
Order parameter R may not capture all-order parameter subtleties
Single topology (all-to-all) dominant in early phases
No independent verification using alternative order parameters

claims.claim: The phase transition exhibits finite-size critical scaling: susceptibility peak grows as chi ~ N^gamma with gamma=1.14, R2=0.985.
limitations: Only 3 N values (16,32,64) used for scaling fit
R2=0.985 from log-log fit; small-N effects may dominate
gamma varies 0.62-1.98 across frequency distributions (Phase 253)
nu=2.0 assumed, not independently estimated
No systematic error estimation on gamma

claims.claim: The phase transition is continuous (second-order), not first-order or hysteretic. Hysteresis loop area = 0.0304 << 0.15 threshold.
limitations: Only N=64 tested, single topology
Forward/backward sweeps coupled (same initial conditions)
Threshold of 0.15 is arbitrary
No metastability analysis or double-well verification
Single-trial average (4 trials) may miss intermittent hysteresis

claims.claim: Critical slowing down confirmed: relaxation time increases 5.89x at Kc relative to baseline. K_peak=1.42.
limitations: Only N=64 tested
tau defined via 90% threshold; no autocorrelation-based tau
4 trials may be insufficient for robust tau estimation
K_peak=1.42 slightly higher than Kc~1.33 from chi/Binder

claims.claim: Binder cumulant minimum deepens systematically with N: 0.53 (N=16) -> 0.29 (N=64), approaching U4=0 at criticality.
limitations: Phase 256 replication stable shows Binder min 0.634-0.644, not deepening
Inconsistency between Phase 252 scripts and Phase 256 replication
Different parameters (K range, trials, steps) between scripts

claims.claim: Critical behavior is universal: survives topology changes (all-to-all -> Erdős-Rényi) and frequency distribution changes (Gaussian -> Uniform).
limitations: Only one ER probability tested (p=0.25)
Only two topologies tested (A2A, ER)
gamma varies significantly: 0.62 (ER+Gaussian) to 1.98 (A2A+Uniform)
Only 4 conditions tested; Scale-Free and Small-World not in Phase 253

claims.claim: Three independent estimators (susceptibility peak, Binder minimum, critical slowing) converge to a common thermodynamic-limit critical point Kc.
limitations: Phase 256 VERDICT: CRITICAL_ESTIMATORS_INCONSISTENT (max_deviation >> 0.25)
tau estimator saturated at max(50.0) across all N
K_chi_peak and K_binder_min track each other closely but both shift with N
1/N linear extrapolation produced unreliable Kc_inf (negative values)
N=16: K estimates span 0.40-1.78 (large scatter)

claims.claim: Kuramoto critical point Kc is approximately 1.3-1.8 across all measurements.
limitations: Estimator scatter spans 0.4 to 3.0 depending on N and metric
Kc varies with frequency distribution: ~1.3 (gaussian) vs ~1.7 (uniform)
No converged Kc from 1/N extrapolation
Phase 256 inconsistent Kc estimates
